summary: Inference platform priced for long-horizon agents and background AI workloads.
editor_note: The bet is that agent infrastructure should price patience, not just speed. Sail shows OpenAI- and Anthropic-compatible APIs, completion windows from asap to flex, model pricing, LoRA/RL rollout support, and Sailboxes for persistent agent compute. Compared with inference vendors racing every token to zero latency, it sells cheaper intelligence when a background agent can wait a minute.

sources.label: Sail seed and Series A announcement

product_evidence.claim: The official site shows OpenAI- and Anthropic-compatible APIs, model pricing, free credits, and completion windows that trade latency for token cost.
Sail describes LoRA fine-tune serving, RL rollouts, usage-based billing, and trillions of tokens served per week as production surfaces.
The Sailboxes docs describe persistent hosted sandboxes for agents that need compute state across long-running tasks.

market_context.primary_user: AI engineers running background agents, deep research systems, evals, and RL sampling workloads.
market_context.differentiation: Sail prices inference by completion windows and pairs it with persistent agent compute, rather than only competing on fastest response time.
market_context.why_now: Long-running agents and eval-heavy development make token economics matter as much as raw latency for many AI teams.

risks.claim: The risk is that developers may still route mission-critical agent steps to lower-latency providers when task timing is unpredictable.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product evidence and funding-source review.
